ageing_drive_cycle.label: Ageing drive cycle
ageing_drive_cycle.intent: Age the cell under an application-like drive-cycle profile and interrupt the campaign at controlled checkpoints for reference characterization.
applications: Accelerated ageing under BEV or PHEV-like duty cycles
Lifetime estimation and BMS-oriented degradation tracking

conditional_required_inputs.note: Use cycle_count to declare how many drive-cycle repetitions belong in each ageing block before the next checkpoint.
Declare the reviewed elapsed-time block in hours or days when the campaign is interrupted on a time basis instead of a cycle basis.

required_equipment: Programmable battery cell tester or cycler capable of dynamic current or power profile replay

steps.name: Select profile family and window
steps.details: Choose the BEV or PHEV / HEV profile family, SOC window, charge regime, block basis, and target temperature before the first block starts.

steps.name: Initial check-up
steps.details: Run the locked baseline reference checkpoint with the declared core RPT bundle before any drive-cycle ageing block begins.

steps.name: Drive-cycle ageing block
steps.details: Run the approved drive-cycle profile for {cycle_count} cycles when block_basis is cycle_block, or for the reviewed elapsed-time block when block_basis is elapsed_time, at the selected temperature and SOC window.

steps.name: Intermediate characterization
steps.details: Interrupt the campaign only at declared checkpoints and run the core RPT bundle first, then add only the selected extension bundles needed for profile-following or impedance evidence.

steps.name: Continue or stop
steps.details: Continue ageing only if the checkpoint template confirms capacity, resistance, and any selected extension outputs remain inside the agreed stop envelope.

data_acquisition: Record the full drive-cycle current or power trace with temperature for every ageing block.
Version each breakpoint decision so the RPT results can be tied back to cycles, throughput, or elapsed days.

graphs: Capacity retention vs equivalent cycles or throughput
Temperature trend during the drive-cycle blocks

planner_notes: The chapter explicitly allows reduced intermediate characterization versus the initial check-up, but the reduced set still has to protect capacity, resistance, and power-capability tracking.

strict_reference_policy.summary: Keep the initial check-up, repeated drive-cycle block, and checkpoint decision loop locked. The exact profile family, breakpoint cadence, and reduced-check-up scope remain review-controlled.
tailorable_fields: BEV versus PHEV / HEV profile choice

campaign_framework.summary: Use the handbook drive-cycle ageing loop as the campaign spine, and use the DOE and periodic-breakpoint literature only to choose a manageable matrix and checkpoint cadence.
recommended_sequence: Screen and pair cells if start-of-life mismatch could mask thermal or profile effects.
Freeze the drive-cycle family and temperature before fine-tuning soft factors.
Run a baseline RPT set before the first ageing block.
Age in repeated blocks and stop only at declared characterization checkpoints.
Escalate to heavier diagnostics at selected breakpoints or end of life.
campaign_framework.replicate_guidance: Use at least two cells per condition when the goal is comparative ageing, and tighten pairing if the campaign is later reused for pack or parallel-string inference.
default_breakpoint_examples: Every 100 cycles when cycle-based control is practical
Every 14 or 28 days when time-based interruption is easier to manage
Final end-of-life diagnostic block after the main ageing threshold is crossed
stop_criteria_options: Capacity at or below 80% of the initial value
Maximum cycle count or elapsed time reached
Bench availability or safety limit reached

reference_check_policy.summary: Keep the initial check-up fixed, then use a reduced but still traceable intermediate RPT set so capacity, resistance, and power capability can be followed through the campaign.

rpt_cadence_mode.selection_rule: Use fixed cycle blocks when the drive-cycle profile and bench schedule allow regular interruptions. Switch to fixed elapsed-time checkpoints when operational scheduling dominates. Bring the next checkpoint forward if the projected fade before the next planned interruption would hide the degradation trend the campaign needs to resolve.

condition_based_trigger.note: Treat the 1% loss trigger as a literature-backed high-resolution example rather than a universal default.

reference_temperature_c.review_note: If the lab standard is 25 C instead, declare that alternate reference explicitly and keep it unchanged across the full checkpoint series.

default_breakpoint_examples: Baseline before the first drive-cycle block
Every 100 cycles or every 14-28 days during ageing
Extended diagnostics at end of life

core_rpt_set.label: Core drive-cycle RPT bundle
core_rpt_set.summary: Run the same capacity-plus-pulse reference bundle at every baseline, intermediate, and end-of-life checkpoint so the ageing observables stay comparable across the campaign.

step_bundle.details: Return the cell to the declared reference temperature and allow thermal and electrochemical relaxation before the checkpoint data run.
Run the standard reference cycle to anchor the checkpoint entry state before the core electrical tests.
Measure reference capacity and energy at the same checkpoint temperature.
Measure resistance and power capability without changing the composition of the core bundle.
Compare capacity and resistance drift against the stop rule before releasing the next ageing block.

checkpoint_extension_tests.label: Profile-following extension
checkpoint_extension_tests.summary: Check whether the aged cell still reproduces the chosen dynamic profile family at selected checkpoints.
checkpoint_extension_tests.selection_rule: Use this extension only at checkpoints tagged for application-fidelity evidence, not at every interruption.

step_bundle.details: Run the reduced dynamic stress test first to verify transient-response drift at the checkpoint.
Replay the representative drive-cycle profile only when the checkpoint is designated for profile-following review.

checkpoint_extension_tests.label: Impedance extension
checkpoint_extension_tests.summary: Add EIS only at selected checkpoints where the campaign needs mechanism attribution beyond the core RPT set.
checkpoint_extension_tests.selection_rule: Reserve this extension for checkpoints where degradation-mode attribution or model handoff is worth the extra interruption time.

step_bundle.details: Run EIS after the core RPT bundle so impedance drift can be interpreted against the same capacity and pulse baseline.
Log whether the impedance result is only diagnostic context or a release criterion for the next ageing block.

checkpoint_templates.label: Baseline reference checkpoint
checkpoint_templates.trigger_rule: Before the first ageing block starts.

step_bundle.details: Execute the locked baseline bundle before any ageing exposure.
Release the first drive-cycle block only after baseline QA and pairing checks are accepted.

checkpoint_templates.label: Intermediate drive-cycle checkpoint
checkpoint_templates.trigger_rule: Stop after the declared cycle or time block, or earlier if the reviewed capacity-loss trigger is hit.

step_bundle.details: Pause only at declared checkpoints so the ageing block and follow-on RPT remain traceable.
Run the unchanged core checkpoint bundle first.
Run the dynamic-profile extension only at checkpoints tagged for profile-following evidence.
Add EIS only at selected checkpoints where mechanism attribution is worth the extra interruption.
Compare the checkpoint outputs against the stop rule and either restart the next ageing block or terminate the campaign.

checkpoint_templates.label: End-of-life checkpoint
checkpoint_templates.trigger_rule: Run when the stop criterion is crossed or the final campaign duration is reached.

step_bundle.details: Run the final core checkpoint bundle at the locked reference temperature.
Run the final impedance extension if the campaign needs mechanism attribution or model handoff.
Freeze the final state with a released end-of-life decision summary and no further ageing restart.

rpt_blocks.label: Core drive-cycle check-up
rpt_blocks.purpose: Maintain the minimum observables the chapter explicitly calls out: capacity, resistance, and power capability.

calendar_ageing_test.label: Calendar ageing test
calendar_ageing_test.intent: Store cells at controlled SOC and temperature for long periods, then periodically interrupt storage for a 25 C check-up protocol.

conditional_required_inputs.note: Declare the storage interruption interval in elapsed time when the campaign uses periodic storage blocks.
Declare both the degradation trigger and the longest allowed interval between check-ups when storage cadence is condition-based.

required_equipment: Battery cell tester for periodic check-ups
Compression plate if cell format requires it

steps.name: Initial check-up at 25 C
steps.details: Run the locked baseline 25 C checkpoint with the declared core RPT bundle before the first storage block starts.

steps.name: Set target SOC
steps.details: Bring the cell to the target SOC at 25 C using the approved reference-capacity logic.

steps.name: Storage block
steps.details: Store the cell at the selected temperature for the declared elapsed-time block, shortening the next interruption if the projected irreversible loss between check-ups would exceed the allowed threshold.

steps.name: Return to 25 C
steps.details: Bring the cell back to 25 C until thermal equilibrium is reached, typically about 5 h, before the residual discharge and next check-up.

steps.name: Residual discharge
steps.details: Discharge to the minimum voltage before the next 25 C check-up sequence.

steps.name: Repeat until stop criteria
steps.details: Repeat the declared storage checkpoint template, including the core 25 C bundle and any selected extension tests, until the duration, SOH, or safety stop criterion is reached.

data_acquisition: Track storage duration, target SOC, storage temperature, and the exact breakpoint at which each check-up was triggered.
Keep charge retention and self-discharge derivations separate from irreversible capacity fade.

graphs: Capacity or SOH vs storage duration

planner_notes: The chapter recommends adapting the storage duration so the irreversible loss between two check-ups stays at or below about 5% capacity.
The chapter also explicitly recommends 2-3 cells per calendar condition for reproducibility.

strict_reference_policy.summary: Keep the 25 C check-up, target-SOC setting, storage block, return-to-25 C equilibrium hold, residual discharge, and explicit stop criteria locked. The matrix breadth and exact periodicity still need review.
locked_planning_fields: return to 25 C until thermal equilibrium, typically about 5 h
residual discharge before the next check-up

campaign_framework.summary: Use the handbook storage-loop as the execution frame, then use DOE logic to keep the SOC-temperature matrix manageable and use periodic check-ups only often enough to protect measurement fidelity.
tailorable_factors: check-up periodicity tuned to irreversible-loss rate
recommended_sequence: Start with the 25 C check-up and target-SOC setting.
Lock the primary storage matrix before expanding optional conditions.
Adjust storage duration so irreversible loss between check-ups stays small enough to interpret.
Use the same core check-up stack at every storage breakpoint.
campaign_framework.replicate_guidance: Use two or three cells per calendar condition when the goal is comparative or model-building rather than a single demonstration.
stop_criteria_options: Visible swelling, leakage, or other mechanical degradation

reference_check_policy.summary: The core calendar-ageing check-up stays at 25 C and repeats before each new storage block. Extend to heavier diagnostics only when the campaign budget supports it.

rpt_cadence_mode.selection_rule: Use elapsed-time storage blocks as the default campaign clock, then shorten the next block whenever the projected irreversible loss before the next planned check-up would exceed the allowed information loss.

condition_based_trigger.note: Keep the irreversible loss between two check-ups at or below about 5% so the checkpoint data stay interpretable.

pre_checkpoint_hold.note: Use the typical 5 h hold as a source-backed example, and extend it if the cell has not reached thermal equilibrium.

reference_temperature_c.review_note: Keep the same 25 C checkpoint reference across all storage conditions and breakpoints.

default_breakpoint_examples: Shorten the interval if irreversible loss would exceed about 5% between check-ups

core_rpt_set.label: Core calendar-ageing 25 C RPT bundle
core_rpt_set.summary: Use the same 25 C core bundle at baseline and every storage interruption so capacity, resistance, and power capability remain comparable across conditions.

step_bundle.details: Bring the cell back to 25 C until thermal equilibrium is reached, typically about 5 h, before the checkpoint sequence.
Discharge to the minimum voltage before the next 25 C check-up sequence.
Run the standard reference cycle to anchor the checkpoint entry state.
Measure reference capacity and energy at the locked 25 C reference.
Measure pulse resistance and power capability at the same 25 C reference point.
Reset the cell to the approved target SOC before the next storage block starts.

checkpoint_extension_tests.label: Impedance extension
checkpoint_extension_tests.summary: Add impedance only at selected storage breakpoints when mechanism attribution or modelling support is worth the extra time.
checkpoint_extension_tests.selection_rule: Reserve EIS for selected checkpoints where degradation attribution or model fitting justifies the extra interruption time.

step_bundle.details: Run EIS after the core 25 C bundle so impedance drift can be interpreted against the same capacity and pulse baseline.
Record whether the impedance result is only diagnostic context or a release criterion for the next storage block.

checkpoint_templates.label: Baseline 25 C checkpoint
checkpoint_templates.trigger_rule: Before the first storage block starts.

step_bundle.details: Execute the full 25 C baseline bundle before any storage exposure.
Start the first storage block only after the baseline results and target-SOC reset are accepted.

checkpoint_templates.label: Intermediate storage checkpoint
checkpoint_templates.trigger_rule: Stop at the declared elapsed-time breakpoint or earlier if the projected irreversible-loss trigger would be exceeded.

step_bundle.details: Terminate the current storage block at the declared breakpoint and preserve the elapsed-time metadata.
Run the unchanged 25 C core bundle before any matrix change.
Add EIS only at selected checkpoints where mechanism attribution or model support is needed.
Reset the target SOC and start the next storage block at the approved condition.

checkpoint_templates.label: End-of-life storage checkpoint
checkpoint_templates.trigger_rule: Run when the SOH threshold, maximum duration, or safety stop criterion is reached.

step_bundle.details: Run the final 25 C core bundle after the last storage block completes.
Run the final EIS extension if the campaign needs end-of-life mechanism attribution.
Freeze the final storage condition, elapsed duration, and stop decision in the release record.

rpt_blocks.label: Core calendar check-up
rpt_blocks.purpose: Track capacity, resistance, and power capability at a fixed 25 C reference point.

constant_voltage_ageing.label: Constant voltage ageing
constant_voltage_ageing.source_title: Constant voltage test (battery maintained at a certain voltage)
constant_voltage_ageing.intent: Hold the cell at a constant voltage for ageing-focused exposure, but keep the current chapter flagged as an outline-only placeholder until a reviewed SOP or richer literature procedure is attached.
applications: Placeholder for future constant-voltage ageing campaigns
Asset slot for a reviewed SOP-backed voltage-hold ageing method

conditional_required_inputs.note: Declare the elapsed-time checkpoint interval explicitly because the extracted handbook chapter does not fix it.
Keep degradation-triggered checkpoints explicitly review-gated until a reviewed addendum defines the trigger logic.

required_equipment: Battery tester with long-duration voltage hold capability

steps.name: Review scope definition
steps.details: Define the target voltage, temperature, hold duration, protection envelope, and checkpoint cadence before execution because the extracted handbook chapter does not provide a complete procedure.
steps.deviation_note: The extracted handbook chapter contains only the heading and preferable-test-schemes stub, so the executable procedure must be supplied by a reviewed SOP or literature-backed addendum.

steps.name: Voltage-hold block
steps.details: Maintain the cell at the approved voltage and temperature for the agreed hold duration while logging current drift, accumulated charge, and temperature.
steps.deviation_note: This block is a planner placeholder until a reviewed constant-voltage ageing procedure is attached.

steps.name: Periodic reference checks
steps.details: Interrupt the hold at declared checkpoints for the approved reference-performance test set and end-of-life decision.
steps.deviation_note: Checkpoint cadence and RPT content are literature-guided placeholders because the handbook extraction does not provide them.

data_acquisition: Log hold current, cumulative ampere-hours, chamber temperature, and any safety or swelling observations throughout the hold.
Keep all breakpoint metadata explicit because the procedure is still a controlled placeholder rather than a finalized handbook execution.

graphs: Capacity or resistance drift vs hold duration

planner_notes: Do not release this as an approved execution protocol until a reviewed SOP or stronger source-backed procedure is attached.
Use the campaign and RPT structures below only as planning scaffolds.

strict_reference_policy.summary: The extracted handbook chapter is not executable on its own. Treat this asset as an outline-only planning placeholder that always requires human review and a reviewed procedural addendum.
locked_planning_fields: reviewed SOP or literature addendum before release

campaign_framework.summary: Use this only as a scaffold for planning discussions: choose hard-to-change voltage and temperature factors first, then attach a reviewed procedural source before execution.
recommended_sequence: Freeze the voltage and temperature matrix first.
Attach a reviewed SOP or richer literature method before execution.
Run a baseline RPT set before the first hold block.
Use repeated hold blocks with declared checkpoints and explicit stop criteria.
campaign_framework.replicate_guidance: Use at least two cells per condition when the voltage-hold study is comparative; otherwise keep the asset in exploratory mode only.
default_breakpoint_examples: Time-based checkpoints such as weekly or biweekly holds
stop_criteria_options: Capacity or energy SOH threshold reached

reference_check_policy.summary: Use the same baseline-and-checkpoint RPT discipline as the other ageing assets, but keep the exact cadence explicitly review-gated until a richer procedure source is attached.

rpt_cadence_mode.selection_rule: Use elapsed-time checkpoints only as placeholders until a reviewed SOP or richer procedure source defines the cadence. If the addendum uses degradation-triggered interruptions, declare the trigger explicitly and keep the threshold under review control.

condition_based_trigger.note: Do not treat these trigger styles as handbook-locked defaults; they stay placeholder options until a reviewed addendum is attached.

pre_checkpoint_hold.note: Keep the checkpoint reference temperature and hold rule explicit in the reviewed addendum.

reference_temperature_c.review_note: The checkpoint reference temperature is a planning placeholder and must be confirmed by the reviewed SOP or literature addendum.

default_breakpoint_examples: Baseline before the first hold block
Weekly or biweekly checkpoints during the hold campaign
Extended diagnostics at end of life

core_rpt_set.label: Core placeholder constant-voltage RPT bundle
core_rpt_set.summary: Keep capacity, resistance, and power capability traceable even while the constant-voltage procedure remains a reviewed placeholder.

step_bundle.details: Return the cell to the reviewed checkpoint reference temperature and allow the declared equilibrium hold before the RPT sequence starts.
Run the standard reference cycle only if the reviewed addendum keeps it in the placeholder core bundle.
Measure reference capacity and energy at the declared checkpoint reference.
Measure pulse resistance and power capability if the reviewed addendum keeps this in the core placeholder stack.
Record whether the placeholder checkpoint outputs are sufficient to release the next hold block or whether the campaign must stop for review.

checkpoint_extension_tests.label: Impedance extension
checkpoint_extension_tests.summary: Add EIS only if the reviewed addendum and budget justify it.
checkpoint_extension_tests.selection_rule: Reserve this extension for reviewed checkpoints where mechanism attribution is important enough to justify the extra interruption time.

step_bundle.details: Run EIS after the reviewed placeholder core bundle when the addendum explicitly asks for impedance evidence.
Record whether impedance is diagnostic context only or a release criterion for the next hold block.

checkpoint_templates.label: Baseline placeholder checkpoint
checkpoint_templates.trigger_rule: Before the first hold block starts and only after the reviewed addendum is attached.

step_bundle.details: Execute the reviewed placeholder baseline bundle before any hold exposure.
Release the first hold block only after the reviewed addendum, protection envelope, and baseline QA are accepted.

checkpoint_templates.label: Intermediate hold checkpoint
checkpoint_templates.trigger_rule: Stop at the declared elapsed-time breakpoint or other reviewed degradation trigger.

step_bundle.details: Pause the hold only at declared checkpoints so the interruption remains traceable.
Run the reviewed placeholder core bundle before any optional diagnostics.
Add EIS only when the reviewed addendum explicitly calls for it.
Compare the checkpoint outputs against the reviewed stop rule and decide whether the next hold block can be released.

checkpoint_templates.label: End-of-life hold checkpoint
checkpoint_templates.trigger_rule: Run when the reviewed SOH threshold, maximum hold duration, or safety stop criterion is reached.

step_bundle.details: Run the final reviewed placeholder core bundle after the last hold block.
Run the final EIS extension only if the reviewed addendum requires end-of-life mechanism attribution.
Freeze the final hold condition, current-drift state, and review disposition in the release record.

rpt_blocks.label: Core placeholder check-up
rpt_blocks.purpose: Keep capacity, resistance, and power capability traceable even while the constant-voltage procedure remains a reviewed placeholder.
